name: stellaris-companion-mcp-relay
description: Connect Claude Desktop to your local Stellaris Companion campaign archive and Chronicle tools.
long_description: Stellaris Companion MCP Relay lets Claude Desktop read your local campaign context from Stellaris Companion and help with strategy, recent events, and Chronicle drafting. Chronicle save, update, create, and undo tools are available only when you explicitly ask Claude to save or apply a Chronicle change back to the app. The relay runs locally over stdio and does not edit Stellaris save files.

tools.name: get_active_campaign
tools.description: Check which Stellaris Companion campaign is currently cached locally.

tools.name: get_strategy_context
tools.description: Read a rich Advisor Briefing for strategy questions about the current campaign.

tools.name: get_recent_events
tools.description: Read recent local campaign dispatches and notable events.

tools.name: get_empire_briefing
tools.description: Read selected sections of the cached empire briefing for follow-up detail.

tools.name: get_cached_chronicle
tools.description: Read the cached Chronicle archive without generating new prose.

tools.name: get_chronicle_source_material
tools.description: Read source material and style guidance for drafting Chronicle prose in chat.

tools.name: save_chronicle_current_era
tools.description: Save a current-era Chronicle draft back to Stellaris Companion after explicit user save intent.

tools.name: update_chronicle_chapter
tools.description: Update an existing Chronicle chapter after explicit user save/apply intent.

tools.name: create_chronicle_chapter
tools.description: Create a new Chronicle chapter after explicit user create/save intent.

tools.name: undo_chronicle_edit
tools.description: Undo the most recent Chronicle edit made through this local MCP relay.

stellaris_companion_path.title: Stellaris Companion app location
stellaris_companion_path.description: Optional. Leave blank for the default install location, or select the Stellaris Companion app/folder. Developers can select a local source checkout for testing.

user_data_dir.title: Stellaris Companion data folder
user_data_dir.description: Optional. Leave blank unless your Stellaris Companion database is stored somewhere custom.

language.title: Language
language.description: Language code for cached Advisor and Chronicle content.
